name: Cranberry Swirl Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds cream cheese, at room temperature, cut into 8 pieces
1 pint sour cream, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium nonreactive saucepan, combine the cranberries and 3/4 cup water. Bring to a boil over moderate heat and boil, stirring occasionally, until the cranberries burst and the mixture reduces to 1-1/4 cups, about 12 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in 1/3 cup of sugar until dissolved. Strain the mixture through a coarse sieve and let the puree cool completely.
2. Preheat the oven to 275 degrees F (135 C). Butter and flour a 9-inch (23 cm) springform pan. In a large bowl, using an electric mixer, beat the cream cheese with the remaining 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons sugar and vanilla at low speed until smooth. Beat in the eggs one at a time, beating until just blended. Stir in the sour cream.
3. Spoon half of the cream cheese mixture into the prepared pan. Drop 8 or 9 rounded teaspoons of the cranberry puree randomly over the top. Spoon half of the remaining cheesecake mixture evenly over the first layer and dot with half of the remaining puree. Repeat with the remaining cheesecake mixture and puree (avoid dropping puree in more than one layer). Using a blunt knife, cut through the batter in a swirling motion to distribute the cranberry puree.
4. Place the pan on a baking sheet and bake in the lower part of the oven for 1 hour. Turn off the oven and leave the cheesecake in for an additional hour. Transfer the cake to a rack and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating overnight before serving.
